quickStart.title: Get productive in 30 seconds
steps: Install: npm i -g @google/gemini-cli or brew install gemini-cli
Authenticate with a Google account or export GEMINI_API_KEY, then run gemini
Use gemini -p "summarize this repo" for one-shot tasks and plain gemini for the full TUI
Hit /plan for read-only planning, /tools to inspect available tools, and /model set gemini-2.5-pro --persist if you want a stronger default model
If you are touching untrusted code, start with gemini -s or set tools.sandbox in ~/.gemini/settings.json

dailyWorkflows.title: Fast one-shot repo question
dailyWorkflows.description: Ask a direct question without opening the interactive UI.
dailyWorkflows.command: gemini -p "Explain the auth flow in this repo"

dailyWorkflows.title: Structured output for scripts
dailyWorkflows.description: Use JSON output so shell scripts can parse the answer, token stats, and tool usage.
dailyWorkflows.command: gemini -p "Generate release notes from git log" --output-format json | jq -r '.response'

dailyWorkflows.title: Plan before changing code
dailyWorkflows.description: Switch to read-only planning mode to inspect a codebase and draft a sequence of edits.

dailyWorkflows.title: Use extra directories as context
dailyWorkflows.description: Bring sibling packages or docs into the workspace context without moving directories.

dailyWorkflows.title: Run in ACP mode
dailyWorkflows.description: Use Agent Communication Protocol mode when integrating Gemini CLI into agentic tooling or orchestrators.
dailyWorkflows.command: gemini --acp "analyze this repository and return a task plan"

powerMoves.title: Create reusable slash commands
powerMoves.description: Store repeatable prompts in ~/.gemini/commands or .gemini/commands as TOML files, including file injection and shell output injection.
powerMoves.why: Turns recurring prompts like commit writing, code review, and changelog updates into one-command muscle memory.

powerMoves.title: Bundle capabilities with extensions
powerMoves.description: Install extensions that package custom commands, MCP servers, prompt context, and tool exclusions.
powerMoves.why: Best way to share team workflows or add specialized commands without manually copying config files.

powerMoves.title: Enable browser automation when text tools are not enough
powerMoves.description: Turn on the experimental browser_agent and optionally a visual model for UI-driving tasks.
powerMoves.why: Useful for sites or flows where DOM text extraction alone is insufficient and you need accessibility-tree or visual browser control.

powerMoves.title: Use MCP servers for custom tools
powerMoves.description: Connect stdio, SSE, or HTTP MCP servers via mcpServers in settings.json and selectively include or exclude tools.
powerMoves.command: # add mcpServers in ~/.gemini/settings.json, then use /mcp list
powerMoves.why: Lets Gemini talk to internal APIs, docs search, issue trackers, or bespoke local tooling without patching the CLI itself.

powerMoves.title: Prefer sandbox plus plan mode on risky repos
powerMoves.description: Combine read-only planning with sandboxed execution for safer exploration of unfamiliar or untrusted projects.
powerMoves.why: Reduces the chance of accidental edits or shell damage while still letting you explore deeply.

gotchas.problem: The browser agent is not available by default.
gotchas.solution: Enable experimental agents and explicitly enable browser_agent in settings before expecting browser automation to work.

gotchas.problem: Custom commands that use !{...} can surprise you with shell execution.
gotchas.solution: Remember Gemini CLI asks for confirmation, keep commands simple, and use argument placeholders carefully because shell blocks escape arguments differently than raw prompt text.

gotchas.problem: OAuth MCP auth needs a real browser on the local machine.
gotchas.solution: Do not expect /mcp auth to succeed from a headless box without local browser/redirect support; authenticate from a local desktop session instead.

gotchas.problem: Project and user settings merge across multiple layers.
gotchas.solution: Check ~/.gemini/settings.json, .gemini/settings.json, env vars, and CLI args when behavior feels inconsistent because later layers override earlier ones.

gotchas.problem: Sandboxing can break commands that need broader filesystem or network access.
gotchas.solution: Switch to a more permissive seatbelt profile, use a container image with needed tools preinstalled, or temporarily disable sandboxing for the exact task.

gotchas.problem: Subagents are experimental and operate more aggressively than normal flows.
gotchas.solution: Treat them as advanced tooling, enable only when needed, and understand the current docs warn they can run in a YOLO-like confirmation model.

cliFlags.description: Run headless with a one-shot prompt instead of opening the interactive TUI.
cliFlags.example: gemini -p "Review this diff for security issues"
cliFlags.useCase: Automation, CI, shell scripts, and fast single questions.

cliFlags.description: Choose the Gemini model for the current run.
cliFlags.useCase: Switch between speed and capability depending on task size.

cliFlags.description: Select response format such as text or json for headless runs.
cliFlags.useCase: Pipe results into jq or other tooling.

cliFlags.description: Add extra directories to the workspace context.
Include all files in context for the current run.
cliFlags.useCase: Broad codebase analysis when you want maximal context.

cliFlags.description: Enable sandboxing for tool execution; can use built-in or configured sandbox backends.
cliFlags.useCase: Safer operation on untrusted code or risky commands.
Fast local workflows when you intentionally want fewer approval prompts.

cliFlags.description: Set approval behavior, including auto_edit and plan-oriented modes.
cliFlags.useCase: Tuning how interactive or hands-off tool execution should be.

cliFlags.description: Run in ACP mode; this replaced the older experimental ACP flag name.
cliFlags.example: gemini --acp "inspect the repo and emit a plan"
gemini -d -p "why is this MCP server failing"
cliFlags.useCase: Diagnosing auth, sandbox, tool, and transport problems.

slashCommands.description: Switch to Plan Mode, a read-only mode for exploring and drafting changes safely.
slashCommands.tip: Great first move in unfamiliar repos; pair it with sandboxing for extra safety.

slashCommands.description: Switch the current model, optionally persisting the choice.
slashCommands.tip: Use flash for quick sweeps, then pro for final reasoning-heavy passes.

slashCommands.description: List, authenticate, enable, disable, and refresh configured MCP servers.
slashCommands.tip: If a tool disappears after config changes, try /mcp refresh.

slashCommands.description: Manage installed extensions from within the CLI.
slashCommands.tip: Extension changes usually require a restart to affect active sessions.

slashCommands.description: Reload custom slash commands from user, project, MCP prompt, and extension sources.
slashCommands.tip: Use this after editing .toml command files instead of restarting.

slashCommands.description: Reload hierarchical GEMINI.md memory and context files.
slashCommands.tip: Useful after updating GEMINI.md or adding context directories.

slashCommands.description: Browse and resume prior sessions or manage manual checkpoints.
slashCommands.tip: All chats are auto-saved, so use this liberally instead of manually recreating context.

slashCommands.description: List, enable, disable, and reload Agent Skills.
slashCommands.tip: If a skill folder exists but does not appear, run /skills reload.

config.recommended: gemini-2.5-pro for deeper coding work, or gemini-2.5-flash for speed-first sessions

config.description: Enable sandboxing and optionally specify the sandbox backend such as docker.
config.recommended: true or "docker" for risky repos and untrusted code

config.description: Additional directories to include in workspace context.
config.recommended: Add sibling packages or docs in monorepos instead of copying files around

config.description: Allowlist trusted tools or shell command prefixes that bypass confirmation.
config.recommended: Only allow low-risk repetitive commands like git status or npm test

config.description: Define stdio, SSE, or streamable HTTP MCP servers and per-server trust, headers, filters, and timeouts.
config.recommended: Keep trust=false by default and include only the tools you actually need
Enable only if you actively need subagents and understand the current safety tradeoffs

config.description: Turns on the browser automation agent.
config.recommended: true only when you need browser-driven tasks and have a supported Chrome setup

config.description: Controls telemetry logging and metrics collection.
config.recommended: Review and set explicitly in team or enterprise environments

proTips: For automation, prefer --output-format json so you get both the response and usage/tool stats in one payload.
Use /plan before giving a broad refactor prompt; it tends to produce cleaner decompositions and fewer accidental edits.
Put team conventions in GEMINI.md and refresh memory instead of re-pasting long instructions each session.
Use project-scoped custom commands in .gemini/commands for workflows you want teammates to share through git.
When an MCP server exposes many tools, filter aggressively with includeTools or excludeTools to avoid wasting context.
